sections.paragraph:
ש·, more rarely שַׁ·, Jud. 5:7 Son 1:7 Job 19:29, and שְׁ

Ecc 2:22 3:18 . Shin prefixed, i.q. אֲשֶׁר, אֲ being rejected by aphæresis, ר either inserted in the following letter, or (in the form שְׁ) also rejected, like הַל, הֲ, but except the book of Judges ( 5:7 6:17 7:12 8:26 ), only found in the later [?] Hebr.

(A) relat. pron. who, which, that, Ecc 1:11 Son 1:7 3:1 2:3 . Often-(a) it is a mere mark of relation, as שָׁם-שֶׁ· whither, Ecc 1:7 Psa 122:4 also, Psa 146:5 Son 8:8.-(b) followed by לְ, שֶׁלְּ (much used by the Rabbins), equally with אֲשֶׁר לְ it marks the genitive. Son 3:7, מִטָּתֹו שֶׁלִּשְׁלֹמֹה “his litter, Solomon’s,” pr. which was Solomon’s; but Son 1:6, there is said with emphasis, כַּרְמִי שֶׁלִּי “my vineyard, mine, I say.” As to the suffix pleonastically used, see Gram. § 119, 2 note.

(B) Conj. relat.-

(1) that, i.q. אֲשֶׁר B, 1, after verbs of seeing, Ecc 2:13 3:18 knowing, Ecc 3:18 Job 19:29 compare Jud. 6:17 Ecc 2:24.

(2) so that. Ecc 3:14, עָשָׂה שֶׁ to cause that.

(3) because that, because, Son 1:6 6:2 for, whence שַׁלָּמָה for why? Son 1:7.

(4) עַד שֶׁ till that, until (followed by a pret.), Jud. 5:7 . כִּמְעַט שֶׁ scarcely that (kaum daß), Son 3:4.

With prefixes-

(1) בְּשֶׁ· i.q. בַּאֲשֶׁר No. 2, because that, Ecc 2:16.

(2) כְּשֶׁ· i.q. כַּאֲשֶׁר No. 1, according to what, i.e. as, Ecc 5:14 12:7 , i.q. כַּאֲשֶׁר No. 3, as, when, Ecc 9:12 10:3 .
